role_title: Director, Restaurant Ads & Promotions - Strategy & Operations - SMB Growth
secondary_archetype: Director-level Strategy & Operations, SMB Growth / Ads

level_signals: Lead a team responsible for setting the strategy, vision and go to market
Manage and develop a team focused on driving growth
Comp range $197,200–$290,000 consistent with Director-level at a large tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 12+ years of experience in consulting, strategy & operations, business development or operations with direct management experience
12+ years of experience in consulting, strategy & operations, business development or operations with direct management experience
You have experience building and scaling products and businesses
You are a proven leader and collaborator and have built, developed and managed high growth teams across an organization
You have a proven track record of leading cross functional initiatives, hitting goals, and succeeding in a complex and fast moving environment
You are comfortable leveraging data and insights to prioritize the strategy

scope.surface: SMB Restaurant Ads & Promotions — product and go-to-market strategy for DoorDash's SMB restaurant advertising and promotions business
scope.business_model: B2B advertising/marketplace (selling ad and promotions products to SMB restaurant partners on DoorDash platform)
primary_metrics_inferred: P&L performance for SMB ads business
core_responsibilities: Develop the product and go to market strategy for the SMB ads business
Work cross functionally with product, sales, finance, marketing, design and engineering to achieve goals
Manage and develop a team focused on driving growth for SMB restaurants
Serve as the trusted voice of the customer across the organization while building relationships with SMB partners

green_flags: Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Clear ownership area: SMB restaurant ads & promotions
Cross-functional scope is well-defined (product, sales, finance, marketing, design, engineering)
red_flags: No location or remote policy stated — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite; candidates cannot assess commute implications
Very broad compensation range ($93K spread) suggests significant ambiguity in level or geography
12+ years requirement combined with 'Director' title may signal an extremely senior bar that narrows applicant pool substantially

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Director' and comp range)
location.type (not stated in JD body; set to 'unclear')
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and SMB context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated; inferred from responsibilities and P&L reference)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Base salary is localized according to an employee's work location')
ambiguities: Work location/arrangement not specified anywhere in the JD — no city, no remote/hybrid/onsite designation

It is ambiguous whether 'experience building and scaling products and businesses' is a hard requirement or a preferred quality — it appears in the 'We're excited about you because' section with no qualifier, so treated as hard
The 12+ years requirement lists multiple domains (consulting, strategy & ops, biz dev, operations) — unclear whether any single domain is required or if a combination counts
